2603.19167.title: Evaluating Counterfactual Strategic Reasoning in Large Language Models
2603.19167.paper_summary: We evaluate Large Language Models (LLMs) in repeated game-theoretic settings to assess whether strategic performance reflects genuine reasoning or reliance on memorized patterns. We consider two canonical games, Prisoner's Dilemma (PD) and Rock-Paper-Scissors (RPS), upon which we introduce counterfactual variants that alter payoff structures and action labels, breaking familiar symmetries and dominance relations. Our multi-metric evaluation framework compares default and counterfactual instantiations, showcasing LLM limitations in incentive sensitivity, structural generalization and strategic reasoning within counterfactual environments.
2603.19167.paper_summary_zh: 我們在重複的博弈論環境中評估大型語言模型（LLMs），以判斷其戰略表現是否反映真正的推理能力或依賴於記憶的模式。<lb/>我們考慮兩個典型的遊戲，囚徒困境（PD）和剪刀石頭布（RPS），並在此基礎上引入反事實變體，改變報酬結構和行動標籤，打破熟悉的對稱性和優勢關係。<lb/>我們的多指標評估框架比較了默認和反事實的實例，展示了LLM在激勵敏感性、結構泛化和反事實環境中的戰略推理能力方面的局限性。
2603.19167.author: Dimitrios Georgousis et.al.
2603.19167.authors: Dimitrios Georgousis, Maria Lymperaiou, Angeliki Dimitriou, Giorgos Filandrianos, Giorgos Stamou
